what_it_does: This file is an IIFE that attaches helper functions to global.DNA.faceRecord. It transforms raw API payloads from /api/sessions, /api/channels, /api/board, and /api/transcript-search into shaped records intended for DNA.renderArchetype. It also includes two small formatting helpers for relative timestamps and shortened paths.
observations: Defines six functions: rel, shortCwd, sessionRecord, channelGraph, boardRecord, transcriptRecord.
sessionRecord builds a facts array and an openLabel, wrapping everything under identity.
channelGraph normalizes channel nodes with score/warmth and builds edges for members, coordinators, and promoted_to relationships.
boardRecord groups board rows by a configurable key and converts the resulting dictionary into a declared array of groups.
transcriptRecord supports two distinct output modes: raw session-star nodes and grounded theme/claim clusters.
Uses global.DNA = global.DNA || {}; global.DNA.faceRecord = Object.assign(...) to merge into a shared namespace.
Comments explicitly state that field-shaping belongs in the DNA/render lane, not in host code.
Human-readable labels are preferred over raw ids/addresses.
standouts: boardRecord explicitly converts a dictionary of groups into an array, because the host should not perform that normalization.
channelGraph accepts members as either an array or an object and normalizes both.
transcriptRecord branches into grounded vs raw modes and carries provenance plus a degraded flag.
The file documents slot_map binding expectations in comments rather than in code.
conventions: IIFE pattern with window as the global object.
Namespace extension via global.DNA and Object.assign.
Defensive defaulting of arguments with raw = raw || {} patterns.
Functional, side-effect-free transformations of input data.
Comments above each function describe the API shape and the intended render record shape.
concerns: Hard dependency on window being defined, which can break non-browser runtimes.
No input validation beyond truthiness checks; malformed nested objects could throw.
rel uses a simple regex that may not handle all ISO timestamp variants.
shortCwd only splits on / and may mis-handle Windows-style paths.
No module exports, making unit testing or bundling less straightforward.
Mutates a shared global namespace, risking collisions.
notes: A thin adapter bridge that keeps host API-fetch code free of presentation field-shaping by centralizing record normalization for four API surfaces under DNA.faceRecord.
questions: What exact record shape does DNA.renderArchetype expect for each archetype?
How are opts.visualDevice and DNA.org.interfaceFragment implemented?
What consumes the edges produced by channelGraph?
What is the 'pack render-map' referenced for boardRecord?
How is the degraded flag intended to affect rendering?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it turns raw route records into shaped records for renderArchetype and keeps field-shaping in the DNA lane.
apparent_themes: Defensive normalization of heterogeneous backend shapes
intention_signals: The file appears to be establishing a reusable surface layer where host code only fetches raw data and calls renderArchetype, while all presentation-specific shaping lives in DNA adapters.
novelty: Explicit normalization of dictionary-to-array grouping, polymorphic member handling, and a dual-mode transcript adapter with provenance and an honest-degrade flag.
contribution: Provides concrete, file-local transformation logic that converts four backend API surfaces into stable render records and exposes them through a shared DNA.faceRecord namespace for renderer consumption.
summary_for_embedding: face-adapters.js is an IIFE that defines global DNA.faceRecord helpers to convert raw /api/sessions, /api/channels, /api/board, and /api/transcript-search responses into shaped records consumed by DNA.renderArchetype.
intention_for_embedding: To keep field-shaping out of host code by centralizing API-to-render record transformation in the DNA surface runtime.

symbols.does: Formats a timestamp into a simple relative-ish display string.
symbols.description: rel takes a timestamp, replaces the ISO T separator with a space, strips seconds and fractional seconds, and returns a trimmed string. It is a small presentation helper used when the host does not supply a nicer value.

symbols.does: Shortens a cwd path to its last two non-empty segments.
symbols.description: shortCwd splits a path on /, removes empty segments, and returns the last two joined by /. If no segments exist, it falls back to the original value. It is used by sessionRecord to produce a compact location label.

symbols.does: Transforms a raw /api/sessions record into a session-card render record.
symbols.description: sessionRecord accepts a raw session object and returns { identity: { id, name, title, cwd, state, last_activity, summary, facts, openLabel, address } }. It builds a human-readable summary and a facts array from state, cwd, model/id, and last activity.

symbols.does: Transforms raw /api/channels data into a channel-graph render record.
symbols.description: channelGraph normalizes channel entries into nodes with label, score, and warmth, then builds edges for members, coordinators, and promoted_to relationships. It handles members as either an array or an object. The result includes nodes, edges, hub, and identity.

symbols.does: Transforms raw /api/board rows into grouped board-view render records.
symbols.description: boardRecord groups board items by a configurable key, defaulting to type. It converts the internal dictionary into a declared array of group objects, each containing group, count, and items. The result also includes identity and total.

symbols.does: Transforms /api/transcript-search responses into a transcript-constellation render record.
symbols.description: transcriptRecord detects whether the response is grounded (asset/themes present) or raw (results array). In grounded mode it emits theme/claim clusters; in raw mode it emits session star-nodes with relevance and match density. It carries query, mode, provenance, and a degraded flag.
